search_queries_used: site:entnet.org BPPV guideline reassess within one month persistent symptoms fall risk
site:nice.org.uk balance clinic follow-up vertigo guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: BPPV guidance applied to every balance-clinic diagnosis
automatic vestibular manoeuvre, medication, imaging, or neurology referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Balance clinic populations include central, peripheral, cardiovascular, neurological, and functional causes.
No diagnosis, treatment, procedure, investigation, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults in balance follow-up when a BPPV or positional-vertigo context is clinician-confirmed; children and non-BPPV balance disorders need separate evidence.
setting_applicability: ENT, audiovestibular, physiotherapy, neurology, or multidisciplinary balance follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requires UAE vestibular service, fall-risk, rehabilitation, imaging, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official 2017 AAO-HNSF guideline exact action statements were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 update remains the current AAO-HNSF BPPV guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult BPPV assessment section supports interval symptom impact, associated or negative vestibular features, mobility or fall-risk context, and focused clinical assessment for a balance follow-up when BPPV is relevant; it does not cover every balance disorder.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact reassessment section supports concerns, clinician-entered review, safety net, follow-up, and questions without diagnosing persistent BPPV or generating treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.

unresolved_source_gaps: Balance diagnosis, trigger, duration, nystagmus, gait, falls, neurological and cardiovascular symptoms, test findings, treatment, response, rehabilitation, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
